page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the 64-core AWS Graviton processor using the arm64 Neoverse-N1 architecture. Operating at 2.5 GHz, it features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, backed by 256 GB of system memory, which yields a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps under the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench software compilation tests scoring in the top 25%. Cached small-block memory read bandwidth is highly efficient at 3782.85 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more modest, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. The hardware demonstrates strong throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving, while large-block memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression remain in the average tier.

The absence of x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX limits its suitability for specialized vector math or heavy local machine learning workloads, as evidenced by weak large-model LLM inference speeds. However, the combination of dedicated ARM cores, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core efficiency makes it a cost-effective option for scale-out workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton processor built on the arm64 Neoverse-N1 architecture. Operating at 2.5 GHz with dedicated physical cores, it features 256 GB of RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 25 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and exceptional throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks are relatively weak, and the lack of AVX instructions limits complex vector math. This instance offers a balanced, cost-effective profile for multi-threaded workloads, high-throughput web serving, and database applications that benefit from fast local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.16xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.16xlarge features 64 arm64 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage for balanced, multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance combining sixty-four dedicated cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton arm64 cores running at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 256 GB RAM and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-core CPU, Redis, and static web serving benchmark performance
Lower single-core performance and lacks AVX instruction set extensions
Best suited for databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded web serving
